theme: The Metropolitan Theatre and Its Survivor
text:
In the east of Britain stands a grand metropolitan building, a theatre whose standing in the city is immense. Its presidential box, draped in velvet so soft that the softness seems audible, has hosted Arab princes and an exiled Arab emir alike. The clever director, a former broker who left the lab of finance for the stage, likes to refer to the place as a survivor of every divergence in public taste. No. 1 on the playbill this season is a theatrical prelude about crime, exile, and grace.

The lead, a steady veteran of the metropolitan circle, was nearly assassinated years ago by a bullet meant for another; he barely escaped, and the coincidence still gives him a chill. Now he rehearses with immense focus, refusing to indulge in the snobbery and fuss of younger players. "Respect the text," he tells them, an assertion he repeats invariably. He will tolerate a missed cue, but not a gloomy, prosaic delivery that drains the drama of its charm. When a scene goes flat, he does not bang the table; he simply asks them to fill the silence with feeling.

The play itself is enjoyable yet evident in its message. A corporate tyrant tries to levy a cruel tax and oust a beloved governor; the people, restless dwellers of a poor district, muster their courage. The plot may seem superfluous at the radius of a single inch, but it slowly will evolve into a study of how grief and an insult can brew into rebellion. A profile of the villain emerges: timid in private, immense in cruelty in public. The cellular detail of each character, down to the smallest nuance, makes the whole feel solid.

Backstage, an old grandfather who has swept the floors for decades cheers every survivor of opening night. He fills a glass of brew, dots the program with a pencil, and watches the velvet curtain glide up. A stream of applause, an ovation, rises through the theatre. The scarcity of such joy in ordinary life is what keeps him here, year after year. To him the standing ovation is no superfluous ritual but a kind of therapy, an invariably honest sculpture of human feeling, carved fresh each evening from the immense and steady love of the crowd. Each theatre dweller and a regular smoker of a classmate from his youth often linger after the show. This player was once a dull, thirsty understudy whose mien betrayed deep disappointment; a rift with the orthodox, possessive old guard had nearly ended his involvement in the craft. But he refused to dissatisfy his own ambition. After a minor incision near his larynx and a long recovery, breathing helium-light and easy again, he learned to handle a role with fluid grace, to cheer rather than agitate a nervous cast, and to treat improvisation as a sacred prerogative. At a plenary rehearse before opening, the majority of the cast gathered around a buffet of ham sandwiches, nickel coffee urns, and whiting fish; they would dot napkins with notes, knot a rope of cues, and measure each pause to the millimetre. Some grumbled the overdue invention of a new lighting rig was an unwanted entertainment expense, but no one stayed dissatisfied for long. A landed patron, born of a sailing family, had funded it. By curtain the whole troupe felt, against every disappointment, that art itself could assassinate despair.
translation:
在英国东部矗立着一座宏伟的大都市建筑——一座剧院，它在这座城市中的地位极其崇高。它那间总统包厢用天鹅绒装饰，绒面柔软得仿佛那柔和都听得见，曾接待过阿拉伯王子，也接待过流亡的阿拉伯酋长。那位聪明的导演曾是经纪人，离开金融的实验室转投舞台，喜欢把这地方称作公众品味每一次分歧中的幸存者。本季节目单上的头号节目（No. 1）是一出关于罪行、流放与恩典的戏剧序幕。

男主角是大都市圈里一位沉稳的老手，多年前曾险些被一颗本要射向他人的子弹暗杀；他勉强逃脱，那场巧合至今仍让他感到一阵寒意。如今他以极大的专注排练，拒绝沉溺于年轻演员们的势利与小题大做。“尊重剧本，”他告诉他们，这是他始终如一重复的一句断言。他能容忍漏接的提示，却不能容忍那种郁闷、平淡的表演，把戏剧的魅力抽干。当一场戏演砸了，他不会猛拍桌子；他只是请他们用情感去填补那片沉默。

这出戏本身令人愉快，寓意也很明显。一个公司暴君企图征收残酷的税，并驱逐一位深受爱戴的总督；那些焦躁不安、住在贫困区的居民鼓起了勇气。情节在一英寸半径的范围里或许显得多余，但它慢慢演变为一项研究：悲痛与一次侮辱如何酝酿成反叛。反派的轮廓逐渐浮现：私下里胆怯，公开场合却残忍至极。每个角色细致入微的刻画，乃至最微小的细微差别，都让整体显得扎实。

后台，一位扫了几十年地的老祖父，为首演之夜的每一位幸存者欢呼。他斟满一杯酿造的酒，用铅笔在节目单上点点画画，望着天鹅绒幕布滑升起来。一股掌声、一阵热烈的欢呼，在剧院里升腾。普通生活中这种欢乐的稀缺，正是让他年复一年留在这里的原因。对他而言，那全场起立的喝彩绝非多余的仪式，而是一种治疗，一座始终如一、诚实的人类情感的雕塑，每晚都从观众那巨大而稳定的爱中重新雕刻而成。剧院的常客，还有他年少时一位常抽烟的同班同学，常在散场后流连不去。这位演员曾是个迟钝、干渴的替角，神态里透着深深的失望；他与正统、占有欲极强的旧派之间的裂痕，几乎终结了他对这门技艺的投入。但他不愿辜负自己的抱负。在喉部附近做了一次小切口、经历漫长康复、重新像氦气般轻松地呼吸之后，他学会以流畅而优雅的方式驾驭角色，去鼓舞而非激怒紧张的同伴，并把即兴发挥视为一种神圣的特权。在开演前的全体彩排上，大多数演员围在自助餐桌旁，桌上摆着火腿三明治、镍质咖啡壶和白粉鱼；他们用便签点缀餐巾，把提示像绳子般串起，把每个停顿精确到毫米。有人抱怨那套迟来的新灯光装置发明是一笔多余的娱乐开支，但没人不满太久。一位出身航海世家、已卸货上岸的赞助人出资修建了它。到落幕时，整个剧团感到，纵有种种失望，艺术本身也能刺杀绝望。
